items.title: Smallholders welcome hike in rubber replanting assistance rate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government's move to increase the assistance rate under the Rubber Replanting Programme has been welcomed by smallholders facing challenges related to costs and uncertainty in commodity prices. In PAHANG , rubber smallholder Adnan Husin, 54, said the increase in assistance eases the burden on the group in carrying out replanting activities and improving yields in the future. "Thankfully, with the increase in government assistance, my plan to plant new rubber trees has been made easier," he said.

items.title: RM15.5bil recovery an unmatched milestone in nation's history - Anwar
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The government has recovered RM15.5 billion in funds and assets over recent years through firm anti-corruption enforcement, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Speaking at the Kelantan PKR Mini Convention here, Anwar, who is also PKR president, said the unprecedented recovery was the result of decisive action to curb leakages and corruption in the country. Also present were PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh and Kelantan PKR chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Supardi Md Noor.

items.title: Come contest in Kedah, says Sanusi to KJ
items.summary: "Yes, anyone can come and contest in Kedah. Come and contest in Kedah — let's see what the people of Kedah say. Kedah is open to everyone.

items.title: App for Rahmah Madani sales info
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has introduced the Info Jualan Rahmah Madani application (app) to provide the public with faster and more accurate information on the programme. Deputy Minister Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said the app enables users to check locations, dates and programme details in real time, helping them plan daily spending more effectively. In a related development, Fuziah said 7,320 series of the Jualan Rahmah Madani programme were held nationwide from Jan 1 to March 31 this year, reflecting the government's continued commitment to helping the people cope with rising living costs.

items.title: What you need to know: Why Malaysia still imports oil and how the Hormuz disruption is driving up costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysia produces crude oil, but it still relies heavily on imports to meet domestic fuel demand that is nearly double local output, while global supply disruptions are also pushing up costs across the oil supply chain. Malaysia continues to operate a “sell high, buy low” strategy, exporting higher-value crude while importing other grades of oil to ensure sufficient supply for domestic consumption. Why does Malaysia still import oil despite being a producer?

items.title: Caltex Malaysia announces temporary fuel supply disruption in five states due to port delay
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Caltex Malaysia has announced that several of its service stations across five states may experience temporary disruptions in the supply of RON95 petrol and diesel over the coming days, beginning yesterday. In an Instagram post, Caltex stated that the affected states are Perlis, Kedah, Penang, Perak, and Kelantan. Caltex also apologised for any inconvenience caused and thanked customers for their patience and understanding.

items.title: Teacher arrested for allegedly molesting special needs student in Jasin
items.summary: JASIN: A teacher has been arrested for allegedly molesting a 15-year-old special needs student at a secondary school here last Thursday. Sources said the victim was in the staffroom with two friends at 1pm on April 16 when the 60-year-old male teacher approached her. The victim and her family lodged a police report following the ordeal, which led to the teacher's arrest.

items.title: BN to maintain seat distribution formula for next state, general elections - Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) will maintain the existing seat distribution formula to face the upcoming state elections and general election (GE), said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir would be tasked with examining the need to make adjustments or increase the number of seats for coalition partners, including 'Friends of BN'. Also present were Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, Kimma president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader, senior leaders of component parties, friends of BN, as well as 938 delegates involving 95 divisions from across the country.

items.title: Kelantan development allocation raised to RM8bil, says Anwar
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Development allocation for Kelantan has been increased from RM5 billion to RM8 billion, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today. Speaking at the PKR mini convention here, Anwar, who is also PKR president, said the additional funding reflected the Madani government's commitment to addressing development gaps and improving the well-being of the people in the state. "I have come to Kelantan many times, but this time I see a stronger determination among the people.

items.title: Firemen struggle to douse 20ha peat swamp fire in Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN: Firefighters are concentrating on buffer zones between a forest reserve and residential areas to control a peat swamp forest fire covering 20ha near Kampung Rhu Batil, Kuala Abang. Dungun Fire and Rescue Station chief Deputy Fire Superintendent II Yahaya Hanafi said the strategy was taken to prevent the fire, which started last Saturday, from spreading to nearby areas. He said firefighters used water from nearby ditches and disused mines to fight the fire.

items.title: Anwar approves RM500,000 aid for fire-hit Bachok religious school
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: The federal government has approved RM500,000 in immediate aid for Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Ulum Al-Muhammadiah in Kampung Kolam, Bachok, following a fire on April 12. Perdana Menteri Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the allocation is intended to repair and restore the school's facilities. Also present were Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

items.title: Malaysia link emerges in sexual abuse and conversion scandal at global IT giant TCS in India
items.summary: NASHIK (India), April 18 — Authorities in India have uncovered a Malaysia-based link in a scandal involving allegations of sexual harassment and forced religious conversion at global IT services giant Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). Digital evidence from WhatsApp chats and video calls linked “Imran” to the wider network are currently under investigation. Indian police said the investigation began after a covert operation in which female officers posed as cleaning staff to gather evidence inside the premises.

items.title: LTSIP runway extension ready by 2027, poised to unlock long-haul travel potential
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said international flight operations do not need to wait until the runway is completed, as the existing 2,400m runway is already sufficient for flights to Asean countries such as Indonesia and Thailand, which typically take between two and three hours. Loke said the ministry will continue discussions with local airlines such as Malaysia Airlines, Batik Air and AirAsia to increase flight frequencies, while also welcoming the entry of international carriers into LTSIP. "Currently, Malaysia Airlines, AirAsia, Firefly and Batik Air Malaysia operate flights to Kota Bharu, with a total of 125 weekly flights covering six key destinations, namely Kuala Lumpur, Kota Kinabalu, Johor Baru, Kuching, Penang and Subang," he said.

items.title: KJ vows to help rebuild public confidence in Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin says he returns to the party with no demands, but with a renewed commitment to serve, contribute and help rebuild public confidence in Umno. He was among 6,252 former Umno leaders and members readmitted under the Rumah Bangsa initiative. Khairy, the former Rembau member of parliament, also expressed his appreciation to the Umno president and the party's Supreme Council for accepting his application to rejoin the party, saying the decision was made with wisdom and consideration for the party's future.

items.title: Malaysia stands firm in speaking truth on global stage - Anwar
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Malaysia remains firm in its independent and sovereign position to speak the truth at the international level, even if it is difficult for some countries and major world powers to accept, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said this courageous stance is reflected in Malaysia's position on the West Asian conflict, particularly the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran. "In my discussion with the President of Iran (Masoud Pezeshkian), he expressed appreciation to the federal government and the Malaysian Parliament for condemning the attacks by Israel and the United States, and for giving strong support in safeguarding the right to freedom in Iran.

items.title: Zahid: Postponing Melaka, Johor polls is a personal view
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has clarified that the suggestion to postpone the Melaka and Johor state elections is merely a personal view. "That is his personal view and not the stand of Umno or Barisan Nasional," Zahid told reporters after officiating the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) 46th annual general meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today. Yesterday, Zahid said BN must retain its two-thirds majority in Melaka to ensure continuity in the state administration.

items.title: Malaysia's 'plastic tide' now a threat to health and food security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's growing "plastic tide" has escalated from an environmental concern into a direct threat to public health and food security, amid rising evidence of microplastic contamination in the food chain. Its chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said findings indicating Malaysians could ingest up to 2,016 microplastic particles annually served as a "grim validation" of long-standing concerns. Lee also highlighted growing distress among coastal fishing communities, particularly in Perak and Penang, where fishermen had reported up to a 40 per cent drop in catches, alongside increasing sightings of diseased marine life.

items.title: Govt approves additional RM20 million for poverty eradication in Kelantan
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: The federal government has approved an additional RM20 million allocation for poverty eradication programmes in Kelantan. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the special additional allocation is specifically intended to assist those living below the poverty line. Anwar said that while poverty eradication programmes were already underway in the state, the menteri besar had requested additional support.

items.title: Hishammuddin: 'I will continue to serve and fight for Umno'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein has pledged to continue serving the party following the lifting of his suspension and his readmission into Umno. The Sembrong member of parliament said he remained committed to his responsibilities and principles after the party's Supreme Council decided to lift the suspension. "Alhamdulillah, the Umno Supreme Council has lifted my suspension after three years.

items.title: PM: 85pc of Malaysians paying subsidised diesel rates, including fishermen, school bus services
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Eighty-five per cent of Malaysians are unaffected by market diesel prices because they still enjoy the government’s subsidised rates, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Eighty-five per cent of the people are not using diesel at the market price; they use subsidised prices. “Fishermen use subsidised diesel prices, not market diesel prices.

items.title: Malaysia consumes twice its daily oil production – Finance Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia consumes about 700,000 barrels of oil per day, double its daily production of 350,000 barrels, necessitating continued crude oil imports to meet domestic demand. "Crude oil and fuel supplies are experiencing disruptions and delays. While 48 per cent of Malaysia's crude oil is produced locally, the country relies on imports for the remainder.

items.title: Sabah Health Dept assures patient safety at Queen Elizabeth Hospital
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah State Health Department has assured that patient welfare and safety remain the highest priority in all implementations. "The hospital guarantees equitable treatment regardless of background, with transparent bed allocation based on clinical needs and availability. Since Jan 29 this year, the hospital has implemented the Health Ministry's patient flow management system to reduce congestion at the ETD.

items.title: Digital shift makes unlicensed moneylending syndicates harder to track
items.summary: Rusdi said victims faced serious consequences, including exorbitant interest rates, prolonged debt cycles, threats, and harassment. "In many cases, victims suffer emotional distress, loss of property, and worsening financial problems," he said. Victims lost RM4.02 million in 2021, RM6.96 million in 2022, RM5.93 million in 2023, RM5.30 million in 2024, and RM6.05 million in 2025.

items.title: PKR denies plan to contest solo in Johor polls
items.summary: KOTA BARU: PKR has dismissed claims that it is planning to contest independently in the upcoming Johor state election, saying the reports are inaccurate and misleading. "However, in Johor, the state government is led by Barisan Nasional, making PH the opposition bloc. "In Johor, PH is not part of the state government.

items.title: Apad issues show-cause letter to bus company over dangerous driving video
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) has issued a show-cause letter to Billion Stars Express Sdn Bhd after a viral video surfaced showing one of its express bus drivers driving dangerously. Investigations are being carried out on the bus, registration number BSB 4999, under the Land Public Transport Act 2010 (Act 715). The probe includes potential violations under Section 22(1)(b)(iii) regarding the conduct of public service vehicle drivers and Section 23(1)(b) concerning the failure of operators to prioritise safety.

items.title: PM Anwar says Petronas negotiating with Russia for oil is an option, domestic needs priority over supplying other nations
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) can negotiate with Russia for oil supply as a proactive measure to ensure the country’s energy reserves remain sufficient for public consumption, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said this step can be taken as many European countries, which along with the United States previously imposed various sanctions on Moscow, are now again competing to secure oil supplies from Russia due to current economic needs. Anwar said geopolitical tensions involving Iran, the United States and Europe have a direct impact on the global transport sector as oil prices and fertiliser supplies are affected.

items.title: Two held with 5kg cannabis worth nearly RM400,000
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Two men, including a foreigner, were detained for possession of 5kg of cannabis buds worth nearly RM400,000 here this morning. The two were detained by General Operations Force (GOF) personnel at a roadblock in Serongga. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspects — a local man and a Sri Lankan — were travelling in a Perodua Alza when they were arrested.

items.title: Six Perlis Pas assemblymen demand explanation over sudden state assembly postponement
items.summary: PADANG BESAR: Six Pas assemblymen are demanding an explanation from Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah over the sudden postponement of the state legislative assembly sitting without a valid reason. The sitting was scheduled for April 21 to 23, but state assembly secretary Mohd Anwar Sahul Hameed announced the postponement in an official letter yesterday without providing new dates. Representing the six Pas assemblymen, Shukri expressed disappointment that they were not consulted regarding the postponement.

items.title: MIC still in BN despite letter to PN, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MIC remains a core component party of Barisan Nasional (BN), BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi confirmed. Zahid, who is also Umno president, said that MIC had since denied ever having applied to join the opposition coalition, despite the existence of a letter to Perikatan Nasional (PN). On March 25, MIC deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said the party had decided to maintain the status quo and remain with BN .

items.title: Pakistan's military chief concludes three-day visit to Iran
items.summary: ISLAMABAD, (Pakistan): Pakistan's powerful army chief completed a three-day visit to Teheran, the country's military said on Saturday, having met top Iranian leaders and negotiators as part of efforts to end the Middle East war. Field Marshal Asim Munir met Iran's president, foreign minister, parliament speaker and the head of Iran's military central command centre, the statement said. The visit showed Pakistan's "unwavering resolve to facilitate a negotiated settlement... and to promote peace, stability, and prosperity", the military said ahead of expected US-Iran talks in Islamabad in the coming days.

items.title: BN to refine seat formula but stick to existing framework, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today urged party machinery to begin groundwork immediately for upcoming elections, saying timing is secondary to preparedness on the ground. “Whether state or general elections are held sooner or later is not the issue. We must go out and reach voters beyond our base.” Speaking after attending the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (KIMMA) 46th annual general meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, Zahid said early mobilisation is key as BN prepares for state polls and the next general election.

items.title: Kimma's BN bid lacks unanimous backing, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kongres Indian Muslim Malaysia's (Kimma) bid to become a core component party of Barisan Nasional (BN) remains stalled by a constitutional requirement that demands unanimous agreement from all existing component parties, and that agreement has yet to materialise. "The BN Constitution has not yet been amended because it specifies that 100 per cent of BN leadership, consisting of core party leaders, must approve any amendment to accept a new party. Present were Kimma president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader and BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir.

items.title: 5.0-magnitude quake hits Nagano Prefecture in central Japan
items.summary: TOKYO: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 5.0 struck the northern part of Nagano Prefecture in central Japan today, the country's weather agency said. According to Kyodo News Agency, the 1.20pm quake registered an upper 5 on Japan's seven-level seismic intensity scale in Omachi and occurred at a depth of 10 kilometres, according to the Japan Meteorological Agency. An upper 5 is defined by the agency as a level at which many people find it difficult to walk.

items.title: Trump teases 'good news' on Iran as ceasefire clock ticks down
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said there had been "some pretty good news" about Iran, suggesting optimism about peace talks to end the war in the Middle East, but he said a ceasefire in the conflict may not be extended without a deal by Wednesday. Iran hours earlier had temporarily reopened the Strait of Hormuz following a separate US-brokered ceasefire agreement by Israel and Lebanon. Trump has told Reuters there would probably be more direct talks between Iran and the US this weekend.

items.title: Japan, Australia seal frigate development deal
items.summary: MELBOURNE: Japan and Australia said Saturday they have concluded a contract to jointly develop a new fleet for the Australian Navy based on the upgraded Japanese Mogami-class frigate, Kyodo News reported. The confirmation came when Japanese Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi and his Australian counterpart Richard Marles held talks in Melbourne. Koizumi said during the meeting that he hopes to take Japan's relations with Australia to new heights.

items.title: PM Anwar: Malaysia stands firm in speaking truth on global stage
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Malaysia remains firm in its independent and sovereign position to speak the truth at the international level, even if it is difficult for some countries and major world powers to accept, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said this courageous stance is reflected in Malaysia’s position on the West Asian conflict, particularly the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran. "In my discussion with the President of Iran (Masoud Pezeshkian), he expressed appreciation to the federal government and the Malaysian Parliament for condemning the attacks by Israel and the United States, and for giving strong support in safeguarding the right to freedom in Iran.

items.title: Nearly 6,000 foreign vehicles fined RM2.19mil in VEP crackdown
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has issued 5,984 summonses totalling RM2.19 million since the enforcement of the Vehicle Entry Permit (VEP) at the Malaysia-Singapore land border began on July 1 last year. RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said 82,987 Singaporean vehicles were inspected during this period near the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) Customs, Immigration, and Quarantine (CIQ) complexes, as well as in Taman Daya. Providing a breakdown of registrations, Aedy Fadly said 391,007 VEP Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tags had been issued as of last Thursday.

items.title: Man jailed for using fake payslip to buy products on instalment
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 18 — The Magistrate’s Court here sentenced a 37-year-old man, who used a forged payslip as genuine to purchase electrical items on instalment, to nine months in jail. Magistrate Wan Farrah Farriza Wan Ghazali meted out the sentence to Nikolaus Owin Yohanes Laba after he pleaded guilty to dishonestly using a payslip in his name purportedly issued by an auto accessories company, knowing it to be false, on July 3, 2023, at 12.54pm, at Kwang Woon Trading Sdn Bhd in Taman Indah Permai, Manggatal. Deputy Public Prosecutor Akaash Singh informed the court that Nikolaus had purchased a television and an air-conditioner on instalment from a shop, using the payslip as supporting documentation.

items.title: Man fined RM48,000 for possessing carcasses, live specimen of various protected species
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 18 — A 38-year-old was fined RM48,000 after pleading guilty to a charge of possessing animals from protected species at the Sessions Court here on Thursday. Hendry Rayner Endim, a plantation officer, pleaded guilty after the charge was read before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali. These animals are classified as protected species under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.

items.title: MIC still ‘core’ member of BN, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today asserted that MIC is still a component member of the coalition, amid speculations over its status. “They are still core members of BN,” he told reporters when asked about MIC’s status after officiating at the AGM of BN ally, Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress at the World Trade Centre here. At its assembly last November, MIC delegates voted to leave BN.

items.title: Sudden lane change suspected in fatal Miri crash involving sports coupe and lorry
items.summary: MIRI, April 18 — A sudden lane change is believed to have caused a fatal accident along Jalan Miri Bypass yesterday morning, said Miri District police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah. He said the accident involved a two-door sports coupe driven by a 40-year-old local man and a lorry driven by a 34-year-old local man. The sports coupe, which was travelling from Permyjaya towards Miri Airport, is suspected to have suddenly changed lanes from the right to the left side of the road.

items.title: Fahmi: Govt to streamline anti-scam efforts with new laws, joint agency session soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — A special session to update all relevant stakeholders on current developments related to online scams will be held the soonest, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil had said today. “We have been working closely with the relevant stakeholders on any matters relating to scams and in fact our last meeting was held last week. “It isn’t just fraud, but all kinds of online scams be it investment scams, love scams and many various other types of scams that has been abusing official channels.

items.title: Zahid backs Kimma entry into BN, but says other components must agree ‘100pc’ first
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said he is open to accepting the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) as a full member. He said Kimma supporters need to prove themselves to the BN supreme council and make a formal application. He said the BN must present a cohesive front.

items.title: Kimma renews push for BN inclusion, raises worker shortage woes at AGM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader today renewed calls for the party to be formally accepted as a component of Barisan Nasional (BN), questioning the delay despite decades of loyalty to the coalition. But the question remains — why are we still not a component party?” he said. This is our country,” he said.

items.title: Ready for orders on ministers’ salary cuts, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Cabinet ministers are ready to accept any orders on salary cuts, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the matter was discussed, however it depends on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call. “I’m ready to accept any orders from the Prime Minister.

items.title: Tanker with one million barrels of crude oil from Iraq arrives in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — The Petronas vessel Ocean Thunder has arrived in Malaysia carrying one million barrels of crude oil from Basrah, Iraq. The shipment forms part of a broader supply network managed by the national oil company to ensure stable fuel availability nationwide amid a disruption in the Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia tensions. “Malaysia's fuel supply is drawn from multiple sources across Petronas' wider supply network.

items.title: ‘I’m home’: Khairy Jamaluddin returns to Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Three years after his sacking, Khairy Jamaluddin has been readmitted into Umno under the Gagasan Rumah Bangsa initiative, alongside thousands of other former members and leaders. “I’m home,” Khairy wrote on his official Instagram account last night, seemingly as a reaction to his return to the party, shortly after an announcement was made by Umno secretary-general Datuk Seri Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. Khairy also expressed gratitude to Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party’s supreme council for the decision.

items.title: Investment fraud losses hit RM1.47b, police say victims trapped in fake profits before being hit with hidden fees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Investment scams caused losses of RM1.47 billion last year, involving 9,603 cases, with the Royal Malaysia Police identifying five main methods, most using social media as the primary channel. Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said tactics uncovered included fake companies (clone firms), promises of quick high returns, Ponzi schemes, love scams, and bogus crypto platforms. Rusdi said non-existent investment cases rose sharply from 2024 to March this year, with 6,337 cases causing RM848.6 million losses in 2024, surging to 9,603 cases and RM1.47 billion in 2025.

items.title: Haj season begins: First group of Malaysian pilgrims leave for Medina amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: SEPANG, April 18 — The first group of 284 Malaysian haj pilgrims departed for the Holy Land early this morning from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 4.05 am on Malaysia Airlines flight MH8050. The group comprised 141 and 143 pilgrims from the Alor Setar, Kedah and Sepang departure stations, respectively. “This morning, we saw off 284 of our haj pilgrims.

items.title: Hishammuddin, Khairy among thousands back in Umno, party supreme council opens door for online membership
items.summary: MELAKA, April 18 — Former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin were among those who were accepted back into Umno under the Gagasan Rumah Bangsa initiative. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the matter was decided at the Umno Supreme Council meeting chaired by its president, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi here last night. The meeting was also attended by Melaka Umno state liaison chairman Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh.

items.title: From ‘just-in-time’ to ‘just-in-case’: Countries rethink economic models as global risks rise, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Countries are moving away from efficiency-driven economic models towards resilience, as rising geopolitical uncertainty reshapes the global economic landscape, including trade and investment strategies. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said for decades, global economic expansion had been underpinned by geopolitical stability, allowing businesses to optimise operations and maximise efficiency. However, he said that as geopolitical conditions become increasingly unpredictable and businesses compete to reposition themselves, that approach is no longer sufficient.

items.title: Private sector key to ensuring Madani Economy delivers real impact, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Greater involvement of the private sector and entrepreneurs is vital to ensure the Madani Economy delivers meaningful, sustainable and widely shared growth, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the Madani Economy framework sets out efforts to raise the country’s growth ceiling, uplift the standard of living and drive reforms, particularly in strengthening governance. Earlier, Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, chaired a meeting with Bumiputera investment institutions to refine more effective and targeted measures to advance the Bumiputera empowerment agenda in line with the Madani Economy framework.

items.title: MACC: Top officials from Survey and Mapping Dept to face charges over RM900,000 fund abuse
items.summary: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the two are expected to be charged on Wednesday or Thursday. Meanwhile, Azam said MACC has recovered more than 70 per cent of 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) assets, amounting to RM30 billion out of RM42 billion. On Tuesday, MACC said the four artworks brought back are Composition (1953) by Joan Miró; Maison de rendez-vous de chasse de Henri IV, Rue St. Vincent, Montmartre (1934) by Maurice Utrillo; Etude pour femme couchée (1948) by Balthus; and L’Ecuyere et les clowns (1961) by Pablo Picasso.

items.title: Seven Kajang students to face school action after bullying video goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Seven secondary school students in Kajang will receive disciplinary action by school authorities after being involved in a bullying incident that went viral on social media yesterday. Kajang district police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said the police found a 47-second video depicting a group of students allegedly verbally bullying a female student. The school has also taken appropriate action against the seven students involved,” he said in a statement here yesterday.

items.title: No approval given: UMS rejects claims of involvement in engineering, biomedical conferences
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 18 — Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) today denied involvement in organising a conference that allegedly used the university’s name unlawfully and promoted UMS as the organiser and venue for the programme without permission on several websites. Its Corporate Communication Centre said that the website promotes the International Global Multidisciplinary Engineering Conference (IGMEC) and the International Conference on Biomedical Engineering & Instrumentation (ICBEI), which falsely claim the university’s involvement. “The university authorities also urge the public to report any such activities or suspicious use of the UMS name to the university for verification and immediate action.

items.title: MOF explains why fuel is getting pricier: Insurance up 17 times, shipping costs triple amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Today’s surge in fuel prices is not solely due to higher crude oil prices following the West Asia conflict, but also to increased risks to the global petroleum supply chain, driving up shipping costs and insurance premiums. “Shipping costs have also jumped threefold, driven by rising oil prices and longer travel routes to avoid conflict areas,” the ministry said in a post on its social media page Friday. It said rising costs along the petroleum supply chain and refining, which far exceed the increase in crude oil prices, ultimately influenced retail prices at the pump.

items.title: Suspects shaping public perception to dilute cases, MACC chief says in ‘narrative war’ warning
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Attempts to distort public perception and undermine institutions are emerging as a key challenge in Malaysia’s fight against corruption. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Azam Baki said the media now plays a critical role in countering such narratives. “They create doubt, influence public perception, and repeat unfounded claims until they appear as truth, while factual information is drowned out,” he said at the MACC’s sixth media appreciation event last night.

items.title: In an emergency, don’t Google — dial 999 for faster help
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — In a medical emergency, every second counts; but many Malaysians still waste precious time searching for a hospital number instead of dialling the fastest lifeline available: 999. A single call to 999 immediately activates Malaysia’s integrated emergency response system, dispatching the nearest available assistance – often faster, and at no cost. Why call 999?

items.title: Air rifles, jihad books seized at home of Ulu Tiram police station attacker’s family, witness tells High Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — A police officer told the High Court here, today, that he seized four air rifles, nine Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) pipes and several books, related to jihad, from the family home of the Ulu Tiram police station attacker in Johor. ASP Cassidy Banta, 42, said the items were seized when he led a search at the home of Radin Luqman Radin Imran, 21, in Kampung Sungai Tiram, following the May 17, 2024 attack which killed two police personnel. The fifth prosecution witness said that, upon arriving at the house, the door was opened by the first accused, Radin Imran Radin Mohd Yassin, 64, who is also the attacker’s father.

items.title: Health Ministry earmarks RM3.42m to improve Batu Pahat clinics
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, April 17 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has announced a special allocation of RM3.421 million to upgrade physical facilities and procure medical assets at four clinics in the Batu Pahat district. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the allocation comprised RM500,000 for the Parit Raja Health Clinic, Sri Gading Health Clinic (RM950,000), Parit Yaani Health Clinic (RM1.268 million) and RM685,000 for the Tongkang Pechah Health Clinic. Also present were Health deputy director-general (public health) Dr Ismuni Bohari, state Health director Dr Mohtar Pungut and Sri Gading MP Aminolhuda Hassan.
